title: Second edition of DIA MedTech Conference to focus on safe, smart, and scalable MedTech innovation
summary: DIA (Drug Information Association), a global community of life sciences professionals, in collaboration with the Kerala Medical Technology Consortium (KMTC), will host the second edition of the DIA MedTech Conference […]
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
The two-day conference in collaboration with KMTC will discuss regulatory pathways, clinical evidence, AI-enabled devices, advance manufacturing and export readiness, and most importantly, people

DIA (Drug Information Association), a global community of life sciences professionals, in collaboration with the Kerala Medical Technology Consortium (KMTC), will host the second edition of the DIA MedTech Conference 2026 under the theme “Concept to Care: Safe, Smart, and Scalable MedTech.” The two-day conference will take place on May 21-22, 2026 in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, India, bringing together key stakeholders across the medical technology ecosystem.

Building on the success of its inaugural edition, the conference aims to provide a collaborative platform for regulators, industry leaders, innovators, healthcare professionals, start-ups, and academic experts to explore strategies that accelerate the development, regulation, and global adoption of medical technologies.

“India’s MedTech sector is entering a phase of rapid transformation driven by innovation, digital health, and evolving regulatory frameworks,” said Dr Ashok Swain, General Manager & Executive Director – DIA India.

“The second edition of the DIA MedTech Conference will enable meaningful dialogue among regulators, innovators, and industry leaders to help translate groundbreaking ideas into safe, effective, and globally competitive healthcare solutions”, he added.

The conference is being organised in collaboration with KMTC, a consortium working to strengthen the medical technology ecosystem in Kerala by fostering collaboration between industry, academia, healthcare institutions, and government stakeholders.

The conference will feature keynote sessions, expert panels, and interactive discussions covering critical topics across the MedTech value chain. These include regulatory pathways and market access across India, the United States, and Europe; clinical evidence and performance evaluation; quality and compliance for global markets; and strategies to overcome barriers to international MedTech growth.

C. Padmakumar, Special Officer, Kerala Medical Technology Consortium (KMTC) said, “The DIA MedTech Conference is designed to bridge the gap between innovation and real-world healthcare impact. Platforms like this are essential for bringing together innovators, researchers, clinicians, and industry leaders to accelerate the translation of ideas into impactful medical technologies. By strengthening partnerships across academia, industry, and healthcare institutions, we can build a vibrant MedTech ecosystem that supports innovation and global competitiveness.”

By convening stakeholders from across government, academia, healthcare institutions, and the medical technology industry, the conference seeks to strengthen India’s MedTech ecosystem and support its integration into global healthcare markets.

Palani Palaniappan, Board Member, DIA Global, CEO- Aridica, USA said, “DIA’s mission has always been to advance healthcare through global collaboration and harmonisation through the exchange of regulatory and scientific knowledge, to enable affordable and accessible to reach large masses of the global population. Through DIA India, we are strengthening engagement with one of the world’s most dynamic healthcare ecosystems and enabling dialogue that supports safe, effective, and globally relevant medical technologies.”

As DIA continues its six-decade legacy of fostering dialogue among regulators, industry, and academia, the DIA-KMTC MedTech Conference 2026 aims to catalyse the next phase of growth for the medical technology sector in India and beyond.

Registration for the DIA- KMTC MedTech Conference 2026 is now open. For more information on the conference and participation details, visit the official conference website.
